english: with a sad and gloomy face Glr.; ṅo nág-par gyúr-ba (ngo nag par gyur ba) to grow sorrowful, to turn pale with fright, pain etc.; ṅo bab (ngo bab) courage fails (me); ṅo srúṅ-ba (ngo srung ba) frq. 'to watch the countenance', to pay much or even too much regard to other people's opinions; ṅo dzin-pa (ngo 'dzin pa) Mil. seems to signify the same, and ṅo čógs-pa (ngo cogs pa) the contrary: not to comply with a person's wishes Mil.; ṅo spród-pa (ngo sprod pa) to lay open the features, to show the nature of a thing, to explain; ṅo ₒpród-pa (ngo 'phrod pa) to understand, to learn, in later literature frq.; ṅo śès-pa (ngo shes pa) to know ccap: ṅa ṅo kyód-kyis ma śès-na (nga ngo khyod kyis ma shes na) if thou dost not know me Mil.; with termin. inf.: to know (that something happens); to find out, e.g. by calculation; to perceive; ṅo mi śès-pa (ngo mi shes pa) 1. not to know 2. unacquaintance, ignorance 3. unknown: ṅó-mi-śes-pa-la ldáṅ-ba (ngo mi shes pa la ldang ba) to rise before a stranger; ṅó-mí-śes-pai yul (ngo mi shes pa'i yul) an unknown country Thgy.; ṅo ltá-ba (ngo lta ba) Glr. is said to signify: to submit (vb. n.); *ṅo lén-če* (*ngo len ce*) W. to beg pardon, cf. ṅos bláṅ-ba (ngos blang ba); ṅo ldóg-pa (ngo ldog pa) or lóg-pa (log pa) to turn away, always fig. = to desert, *kór-bai yul ṅo lóg-na* (*'khor ba'i yul ngo log na*) if you will desert, get rid of, the land of transmigration; more frq.: ṅo-lóg byéd-pa (ngo log byed pa) to revolt, rebel, rtsóm-pa (rtsom pa) to bring about a revolt, ṅo-lóg-mkhan (ngo log mkhan) mutineer, agitator, ṅo-lóg-čan (ngo log can) seditious, faithless, ṅo zlóg-pa (ngo zlog pa) reg. to oppose, resist, not comply with a person's wish Dzl. — 2. side, like ṅos (ngos), esp. W.: *'a ṅó-la soṅ* (*'a ngo la song*) he has gone to that side, in that direction; *sám-pa 'a ṅo 'i ṅó-a soṅ* (*bsam pa 'a ngo 'i ngo a song*) he is absent, inattentive. — 3. self, the thing itself, cf. ṅó-bo (ngo bo) and ṅos (ngos); v. jál-ba (mjal ba); also sbst. the self, the I, ṅo-tsáb (ngo tshab) the representative of the I; cf. also ṅo-čén (ngo chen). — 4. likelihood, prospect of, c. genit. inf. or root, ₒkyér-bai ṅo (khyer ba'i ngo) a probability of its being taken away; bu čig ₒbyuṅ ṅo če (bu cig byung ngo che) a great chance of (getting) a son. — 5. (also ṅos (ngos)) a. the waxing and waning moon, with regard to shape; one half of the lunar month with regard to time, yar - ṅo (yar ngo) the former, mar - ṅo (mar ngo) the latter half; yár - ṅo zla ltar (yar ngo zla ltar) like the crescent moon. b. in a special sense the increasing moon, or the first half of the month; thus vulgo; so also in B.: zlá-ba dgu ṅo bču lón-pa-na (zla ba dgu ngo bcu lon pa na) Glr., ṅo bčù-nas, zlá-ba ṅo bču-na (ngo bcu nas, zla ba ngo bcu na) Pth. in the first half of the tenth month (to denote the duration of pregnancy). Comp. and deriv. ṅo dkar (ngo dkar) v. above 1. — ṅo-lkog (ngo lkog) prop. adj.: public and private, open and secret, but it is generally used as a synonym of zol (zol) or rdzun (rdzun), fraud, imposture, deceit, eye-service. It may be explained by its contrary: ṅó-med lkóg-med (ngo med lkog med) acting in the same manner in public as in private life, the open and the secret conduct being alike Mil. (cf. ṅos (ngos)). — ṅó-čan (ngo can) natural (?) Cs. — ṅo-čén (ngo chen) ('the greater self'), a man of influence interceding for another person, an intercessor; ṅo-čén byéd-pa (ngo chen byed pa) to intercede Glr.; mi-la ṅo-čén ₒčól-ba (mi la ngo chen bcol ba) to use a person as negotiator, to make inquiries through him Glr. (Sch. incorr.) — ṅo-rtóg (ngo rtog) W. 1. (like ṅés-pa (nges pa) of B.) certain, e.g. *ṅo-tóg śè-če* (*ngo tog she ce*) to know for certain. 2. (like ṅos (ngos), yaṅ-dag-pa (yang dag pa)) real, actual; true, genuine, *ₒtul yin-na ṅo-tóg yin* (*'khrul yin na ngo tog yin*) is it counterfeit or genuine? illusion or reality? *ṅo-tóg sád-kan* (*ngo tog bsad mkhan*) the actual murderer, he who really occasioned the death. — *ṅó-stod-kan* (*ngo bstod mkhan*) W. he who praises another to his face, a flatterer. — ṅo-nág (ngo nag) v. above 1. — ṅó-bo-ñyíd (ngo bo nyid), entity, ṅo-bo-ñyíd-mèd-pa (ngo bo nyid med pa) non-entity Tar. 90, 2.; essence, nature, substance, e.g. séms-kyi (sems kyi) Mil.; character Was. (278, 291); marrow, main substance, quintessence ( = sñyíṅ-po (snying po)) Glr. and elsewh.; ráṅ-gi ṅó-bos (rang gi ngo bos) in itself, according to its intrinsic nature Mil.; also col. *ṅó-bo ko-ráṅ* (*ngo bo kho rang*) C. the thing itself, opp. to a surrogate; ṅó-bo yčìg rtógs-pa ñyis (ngo bo gcig rtogs pa gnyis) Was.: 'one quality, two (different) ideas' (Schl. has ldóg-pa (ldog pa) instead of rtóg-pa (rtog pa)). — ṅo-ₒbáb-pa (ngo 'bab pa) adj. discouraged, timorous, bashful W. — ṅó-ma (ngo ma) acc. to Cs. = ṅo (ngo). — ṅó-ma-yyog (ngo ma g.yog) C.: master and servant. — ṅó-mig (ngo mig) W. boldness; *ṅó-mig-čan* (*ngo mig can*), or *čén-po* (*chen po*) bold, daring, courageous; *ṅó-mig čuṅ-se* (*ngo mig chung se*) shy, timid, faint-hearted W. — ṅó-tsa (ngo tsha) ('heat of the face') 1. the act of blushing, shame, ṅó-tsai ynas (ngo tsha'i gnas) shameful things Sch., Schr.; ṅó-tsa-čan (ngo tsha can),
